grammarNotes.body: The preposition 以 introduces an instrumental phrase, glossed as "by means of" or "through." In 故經之以五事, 以五事 means "by means of the five matters." This pattern repeats in 校之以計 ("by means of calculation"). In Modern Chinese this would be expressed with 用 or 通過, but Classical Chinese favors 以 for this function.

A four-item enumeration in the pattern NUM曰NOUN, where 曰 functions as a copula or colon marker: "First is called [X], second is called [Y]," etc. The 曰 after each number creates a parallel structure listing the five strategic constants. This construction is typical of Classical Chinese prescriptive and didactic discourse.
